reasoning: The flagged issue in fetch_tencent_wechat_release.sh is a false positive; the script is a maintainer helper not executed during user installation. All user-facing components (PKGBUILD, install hooks, sandbox script) show safe patterns.
disagreement_analysis: qwen incorrectly classified fetch_tencent_wechat_release.sh as unsafe due to processing untrusted archives without prior integrity checks, despite this being a maintainer-only script unused in user builds.
false_positives: qwen's verdict on fetch_tencent_wechat_release.sh: This script is a maintainer tool for updating PKGBUILD metadata, not executed during makepkg. User installations use pre-verified checksums.
learnings: Improve context awareness: Audit tools should better distinguish between maintainer scripts (low risk) and user-executed build/install scripts.
Enhance prompt guidance: Clarify that network-accessing helpers are acceptable if isolated from build functions and used only for metadata fetching.
Prioritize build artifacts: Focus reviews on files referenced in PKGBUILD functions (build(), package()) and .install scripts first.
